content.content:
276	### Prototype adoption — dedicated-page flows + Dashboard landing (added — not in original roadmap) (6 tasks — 0/6) — tracked in epic [#175](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/175)
277	> Adopt the **DataQ Design System** prototype (claude.ai/design project *"DataQ Design System"*, reverse-engineered from the shipped indigo/antd app — its `readme.md` is the spec). The **foundation already matches** the product (`theme.ts` palette, `resultsFormat.ts` status model, the 56px-header/220px-sider shell), so this is **not a re-skin** — it adopts the prototype's **structure**: every create/edit flow becomes a dedicated, deep-linkable **page** (the **Share** drawer stays the *only* drawer), login lands on an **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard**, and standard **error pages** ship. Components are reproduced with **real antd** — the prototype's framework-free recreations + `_ds_bundle.js` are reference only (per the design README). Backend-gated screens (Admin, Settings) + post-v1 items (marketing, dark mode) are tracked in Week 7 + Post-v1 below. The intentionally-dropped prototype bits (the **"View as" role switch**, the **component bundle**, the **teal palette**) are recorded in Post-v1 as *not adopted*.
278	>
279	> **Design source — how to open the prototype (read before picking up any task in this track):** the claude.ai/design project **"DataQ Design System"** — `projectId` **`317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d`** (owner Arijit). Read it with the **`/design-sync`** skill / **`DesignSync`** tool over the claude.ai login: `DesignSync method=list_files projectId=…`, then `method=get_file path=<…>`. Key paths: **`readme.md`** = the canonical spec (voice · color · type · spacing · status model · component/template manifest); **`guidelines/*.card.html`** = foundation specimen cards; **`templates/app/*.jsx`** = framework-free recreations of each screen; **`components/<group>/*.jsx`** = the component recreations; **`tokens/*.css`** = raw token values; **`uploads/DESIGN.md`** = the *superseded teal* brief (ignore for palette). ⚠️ The hosted share link `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/design/h/SVkQJqee7xsc2FNWVOsnIQ` returns **404** — reach the prototype via DesignSync, not the link. The `.jsx` / `_ds_bundle.js` are **reference only** — build in real antd reading `theme.ts`.
280	- [ ] ⬜ **Nav / shell restructure** — add **Dashboard** as the landing entry; reorder the sider to **Dashboard · Connections · Suites · Results · Profile**; add the footer group **Settings · Admin · Documentation** (Admin/Settings land in Week 7). Change the login redirect `/` → `/dashboard` (was `/connections`). Touches `App.tsx` `NAV_ITEMS` + `<Routes>` only — the nav already prefix-matches ([#192](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/192)). _Design ref: `templates/app/AppShell.jsx` (white header + light sider + footer nav) + readme §"What's in here → Templates → `templates/app`" (the sidebar + footer item lists)._
281	- [ ] ⬜ **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard page** (`/dashboard`, `DashboardScreen`) — the new landing screen that **hosts the existing Week-6 dashboard widgets** (health-score stat cards, 7-day quality-trend chart, per-suite performance bars, **recent-runs table** — the task moved from Week 5). This is the **page home** for the "Health score stat cards", "Per-suite pass/fail bars", "Per-check historical trend chart" and "Recent runs audit table" tasks above — build them here rather than on `/results`. **Prereq:** a chart-library decision (antd ships no charts → `@ant-design/charts` or `recharts`; runs through `pnpm audit` + bundle-size gate → ADR-light note). Render only backend-backed KPIs (see the W7 *KPI honesty pass*). _Design ref: `templates/app/DashboardScreen.jsx`; components `data/MetricCard.jsx`, `data/Card.jsx`, `feedback/ProgressBar.jsx`, `data/DataTable.jsx`; readme §"Templates" (Enhanced Monitoring — KPIs · Quality Trends · Suite Performance · Recent Runs)._
282	- [ ] ⬜ **New Suite → dedicated page** (`/suites/new`, `SuiteFlow`) — extract the `SuiteDrawer` **create** path into a routed *details / source* page reusing `suiteTarget.ts` + the run-target editor (PR #246). Removes the drawer's create branch; `Suites.tsx` swaps `setDrawer({open:true})` for `navigate('/suites/new')`. _Design ref: `templates/app/SuiteFlow.jsx` (New Suite → Details/Source) → `CheckFlow.jsx` (Define Rules); readme §"Templates" full-page-flows note._
283	- [ ] ⬜ **Edit flows → dedicated pages** — unify edit onto the existing create pages via an `:id` route + prefill, reusing the already-extracted form specs (`connectionFormSpec.ts` / `checkForm.ts` / `suiteTarget.ts`): connection edit `/connections/:id/edit` → `ConnectionNew`; suite edit `/suites/:id/edit` → `SuiteFlow`; check edit `/suites/:id/checks/:checkId/edit` → `CheckNew`. **Removes** the `ConnectionDrawer` / `CheckDrawer` / `SuiteDrawer` **edit** drawers; the parent pages drop their `editing` / `drawer` state for `navigate()`. (Dedups with [#204](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/204).) _Design ref: `templates/app/ConnectionFlow.jsx` (Configuration), `SuiteFlow.jsx`, `CheckFlow.jsx` — the create flows; edit reuses the same page shape, prefilled._
284	- [ ] ⬜ **Results drill-down → dedicated route** (`/results/:runId`, `ResultsScreen` detail) — promote the in-page `openRun` `useState` ([Results.tsx:54](../frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx#L54)) to a routed run-detail page with back-nav, per-check results (severity tags + `metric_value` + observed), and CSV/JSON download. Deep-linkable + refreshable (the prototype's `ResultsScreen` flow). _Design ref: `templates/app/ResultsScreen.jsx`; readme §"Templates" (Results → click a run → Run detail with per-check results + CSV/JSON download)._
285	- [ ] ⬜ **Standard error pages (4xx / 5xx)** — an `ErrorState`-based page in the app shell switchable across 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 429 / 500 / 502 / 503 / 504; wire it into the router catch-all + the `ErrorBoundary` ([PR #210](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/210)) fallback so render-time + routing failures render in-brand. _Design ref: `templates/error/index.html` + `components/feedback/ErrorState.jsx` (+ `components/feedback/errorstate.card.html`); readme §"Templates → `templates/error/`"._
286	
287	**Week 6 total: 2 / 23** _(early credit: Results page scaffold + severity badge colours (PR-C1, the GX-Cloud redesign Phase C); 3 dashboard tasks partially landed — status/provider filters, per-check drill-down, pipeline-runs feed. +6 prototype-adoption tasks added: nav/shell, Dashboard landing page, suite-create page, edit-flows→pages, results drill-down route, error pages. +1 moved in from Week 5: recent-runs audit table — folded into the Dashboard page.)_

content.text: The Admin/Settings screens were previously parked in Week 7's prototype-adoption set, so pulling them into Week 6 affects both weeks' counts. Let me read the Week-7 section to move them accurately rather than guess.

input.command: grep -n "Settings\|Admin\|prototype\|Profile\|backend-gated\|Week 7 total\|^### \|^## Week 7" docs/progress.md | sed -n '1,60p'
input.description: Find Week 7 prototype-adoption tasks and section markers

content.content:
26:| **Week-5 exit gate** | Async runs with live progress across all datasource types; scheduling operational. — **MET**: run paths across all datasource types done (Snowflake + flat-file batch + UC), run **progress** + **cancel** APIs shipped + the **live run-progress UI (A3)** consuming them, `error`/`skip` operational statuses landed, orchestration `/pipelines` + gap recovery + **scheduling backend (A7)** + the **scheduled-runs UI (A6)** in (scheduling operational end-to-end), **run-history retention sweep (A8)** done, **run-now panel** shipped. The one roadmap Execution-UI task not built — the **recent-runs audit table** — was deliberately **moved to Week 6** to be built once in its prototype page-home (`/dashboard`), not a gate item. Weeks 1–4 exit gates also met — see [CLAUDE.md §13](../CLAUDE.md). Live warehouse/file run remains the deferred Week-1 smoke. |
27:| **Next milestone** | **Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting** (early credit 2/23: Results page scaffold + severity badges, PR-C1). Up next: health-score stat cards + 7-day trend chart, per-suite pass/fail bars, the prototype **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard** landing page (`/dashboard`, hosting the moved-in recent-runs table) — gated on a chart-library decision — plus the `ResultPublisher` alerting seam + Teams notifications. |
45:### Live test/demo-data environment (v1-supporting harness — lives _outside_ this repo, ADR [0021](adr/0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md))
65:### Setup (4 tasks — 4/4 ✅)
71:### Auth (3 tasks — 1.5/3 ✅, 1.5/3 🟡)
76:### GX baseline & API conventions (3 tasks — 2/3 ✅, 1/3 🟡)
91:### Snowflake & ADF (4 tasks — 4/4)
97:### ADF webhook receiver (Azure Monitor → DQ platform) (5 tasks — 4 ✅ / 1 🟡)
104:### Airflow orchestration (added per ADR 0004; not in original roadmap) (3 tasks — 3/3)
109:### Flat file — ADLS Gen2 & S3 (4 tasks — 2/4)
115:### Unity Catalog / Databricks (3 tasks — 2/3)
128:### Suite & check backend (4 tasks — 4/4 ✅)
134:### Severity threshold tiers (warn / fail / critical) (4 tasks — 4/4 ✅)
141:### Monitor abstraction & metric storage — do-now seams (3 tasks — 3/3 ✅)
147:### Column profiler (3 tasks — 3/3 ✅)
149:- [x] ✅ Column profiler endpoint (ADLS / S3) — same stats via Pandas on sampled file — extended `profile_service` into a type dispatcher (`profile_connection`): SQL types → in-warehouse aggregation (unchanged), flat-file types (`adls_gen2`/`s3`) → download a **sample** of the file into Pandas and compute the same stats (`profile_dataframe`). Same `POST /suites/{id}/profile` endpoint, now polymorphic: SQL targets pass `table`/`schema`, flat-file targets pass `path` (+ optional `file_format`, else inferred from extension); response carries whichever identity applies. CSV + Parquet. **pandas "load less data" levers** (per the user's scale.html prompt): column projection (CSV `usecols`, Parquet `columns=` via pyarrow schema) so profiling 3 of N columns doesn't read all N, + row sampling (`_SAMPLE_ROWS`), + **zero-copy Arrow-backed dtypes** on the Parquet read (`dtype_backend="pyarrow"` — parquet is already Arrow, kept zero-copy; the stat helpers + `_to_native` are Arrow-scalar/`pd.NA`-safe). Stats are vectorised pandas (no Python row loops — enhancingperf N/A; CoW is a no-op for a read-only profiler + a process-global flag, so not toggled; pyarrow CSV engine rejected — it's incompatible with `nrows`/callable-`usecols`). NaN/Timestamp→JSON-safe. v1 → 422: unsupported type (UC still pending), missing target, unknown format, missing column; read failure → 502 (exception never echoed). Reuses `ColumnProfile`/`ProfileResult`. 24 new tests (pure `profile_dataframe`/`infer_file_format`/`_to_native`/projection-parse + endpoint via fake `_read_dataframe`); `suites.py` 100%, `profile_service` 85% (only the live `_open_connection`/`_download_bytes` network seams uncovered — deferred smoke). _Deferred: streaming/range reads (whole object still downloaded before sampling) + out-of-core (Dask) — future work if profiling cost bites._
152:### Flat file check specifics (2 tasks — 2/2 ✅)
156:### Unity Catalog check specifics (2 tasks — 2/2 ✅)

277:> Adopt the **DataQ Design System** prototype (claude.ai/design project *"DataQ Design System"*, reverse-engineered from the shipped indigo/antd app — its `readme.md` is the spec). The **foundation already matches** the product (`theme.ts` palette, `resultsFormat.ts` status model, the 56px-header/220px-sider shell), so this is **not a re-skin** — it adopts the prototype's **structure**: every create/edit flow becomes a dedicated, deep-linkable **page** (the **Share** drawer stays the *only* drawer), login lands on an **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard**, and standard **error pages** ship. Components are reproduced with **real antd** — the prototype's framework-free recreations + `_ds_bundle.js` are reference only (per the design README). Backend-gated screens (Admin, Settings) + post-v1 items (marketing, dark mode) are tracked in Week 7 + Post-v1 below. The intentionally-dropped prototype bits (the **"View as" role switch**, the **component bundle**, the **teal palette**) are recorded in Post-v1 as *not adopted*.
279:> **Design source — how to open the prototype (read before picking up any task in this track):** the claude.ai/design project **"DataQ Design System"** — `projectId` **`317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d`** (owner Arijit). Read it with the **`/design-sync`** skill / **`DesignSync`** tool over the claude.ai login: `DesignSync method=list_files projectId=…`, then `method=get_file path=<…>`. Key paths: **`readme.md`** = the canonical spec (voice · color · type · spacing · status model · component/template manifest); **`guidelines/*.card.html`** = foundation specimen cards; **`templates/app/*.jsx`** = framework-free recreations of each screen; **`components/<group>/*.jsx`** = the component recreations; **`tokens/*.css`** = raw token values; **`uploads/DESIGN.md`** = the *superseded teal* brief (ignore for palette). ⚠️ The hosted share link `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/design/h/SVkQJqee7xsc2FNWVOsnIQ` returns **404** — reach the prototype via DesignSync, not the link. The `.jsx` / `_ds_bundle.js` are **reference only** — build in real antd reading `theme.ts`.

147:### Column profiler (3 tasks — 3/3 ✅)
149:- [x] ✅ Column profiler endpoint (ADLS / S3) — same stats via Pandas on sampled file — extended `profile_service` into a type dispatcher (`profile_connection`): SQL types → in-warehouse aggregation (unchanged), flat-file types (`adls_gen2`/`s3`) → download a **sample** of the file into Pandas and compute the same stats (`profile_dataframe`). Same `POST /suites/{id}/profile` endpoint, now polymorphic: SQL targets pass `table`/`schema`, flat-file targets pass `path` (+ optional `file_format`, else inferred from extension); response carries whichever identity applies. CSV + Parquet. **pandas "load less data" levers** (per the user's scale.html prompt): column projection (CSV `usecols`, Parquet `columns=` via pyarrow schema) so profiling 3 of N columns doesn't read all N, + row sampling (`_SAMPLE_ROWS`), + **zero-copy Arrow-backed dtypes** on the Parquet read (`dtype_backend="pyarrow"` — parquet is already Arrow, kept zero-copy; the stat helpers + `_to_native` are Arrow-scalar/`pd.NA`-safe). Stats are vectorised pandas (no Python row loops — enhancingperf N/A; CoW is a no-op for a read-only profiler + a process-global flag, so not toggled; pyarrow CSV engine rejected — it's incompatible with `nrows`/callable-`usecols`). NaN/Timestamp→JSON-safe. v1 → 422: unsupported type (UC still pending), missing target, unknown format, missing column; read failure → 502 (exception never echoed). Reuses `ColumnProfile`/`ProfileResult`. 24 new tests (pure `profile_dataframe`/`infer_file_format`/`_to_native`/projection-parse + endpoint via fake `_read_dataframe`); `suites.py` 100%, `profile_service` 85% (only the live `_open_connection`/`_download_bytes` network seams uncovered — deferred smoke). _Deferred: streaming/range reads (whole object still downloaded before sampling) + out-of-core (Dask) — future work if profiling cost bites._

- [ ] ⬜ **New Suite → dedicated page** (`/suites/new`, `SuiteFlow`) — extract the `SuiteDrawer` **create** path into a routed *details / source* page reusing `suiteTarget.ts` + the run-target editor (PR #246). content.text: Now add the four newly-tracked screens after the error-pages task, and update the Week-6 total:

input.old_string:
- [ ] ⬜ **Standard error pages (4xx / 5xx)** — an `ErrorState`-based page in the app shell switchable across 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 429 / 500 / 502 / 503 / 504; wire it into the router catch-all + the `ErrorBoundary` ([PR #210](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/210)) fallback so render-time + routing failures render in-brand. _Design ref: `templates/error/index.html` + `components/feedback/ErrorState.jsx` (+ `components/feedback/errorstate.card.html`); readme §"Templates → `templates/error/`"._

**Week 6 total: 2 / 23** _(early credit: Results page scaffold + severity badge colours (PR-C1, the GX-Cloud redesign Phase C); 3 dashboard tasks partially landed — status/provider filters, per-check drill-down, pipeline-runs feed. +6 prototype-adoption tasks added: nav/shell, Dashboard landing page, suite-create page, edit-flows→pages, results drill-down route, error pages. +1 moved in from Week 5: recent-runs audit table — folded into the Dashboard page.)_
input.new_string:
- [ ] ⬜ **Standard error pages (4xx / 5xx)** — an `ErrorState`-based page in the app shell switchable across 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 429 / 500 / 502 / 503 / 504; wire it into the router catch-all + the `ErrorBoundary` ([PR #210](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/210)) fallback so render-time + routing failures render in-brand. _Design ref: `templates/error/index.html` + `components/feedback/ErrorState.jsx` (+ `components/feedback/errorstate.card.html`); readme §"Templates → `templates/error/`"._
- [ ] ⬜ **New Connection → source-select + config (2-page flow)** — adopt the prototype's two-step New-Connection: a categorized **Select Source** page (**Orchestration first**, then Warehouses / Lakehouses / Cloud Storage) → the spec-driven **Configuration** page. Verify `ConnectionNew` (`/connections/new`) splits the source pick into its own step rather than one combined page. _Design ref: `templates/app/ConnectionFlow.jsx` (`ConnectionSourceScreen` → `ConnectionConfigScreen`); `connectionFormSpec.ts`; readme §"Templates" full-page-flows note._
- [ ] ⬜ **Profile page — real content** (`/profile`, `ProfileScreen`) — replace the placeholder `Home` ([App.tsx:100](../frontend/src/App.tsx#L100)) with the prototype's account screen: identity card (avatar, name, email, role tag) + workspace facts (workspace, auth = Azure AD/MSAL, suite access, member-since) on `/me`, and an **Alert channels** card whose toggles front the Week-6 `ResultPublisher` notification config (Teams etc.). Render only `/me`-backed facts (KPI-honesty). _Design ref: `templates/app/ProfileScreen.jsx`._
- [ ] ⬜ **Workspace Settings page** (`/settings`, `SettingsScreen`) — _(moved in from Week 7)_ — tabbed shell: **General · Secrets (Key Vault) · Notifications · Danger zone**. **No settings/preferences backend exists today** (only the `v1/` routers), so v1 ships the page shell + only controls that map to a real backend (e.g. the Teams webhook config once the W6 `ResultPublisher` notification UI lands); everything else renders as a clearly-labelled placeholder. **No hardcoded Azure resource names** — Key Vault state via the generic secret seam (ADR 0010/0013). _Design ref: `templates/app/SettingsScreen.jsx`._
- [ ] ⬜ **Admin page — reconcile layout to prototype** — the Admin Control Center shipped (#289, Week 4) with Suites / Users / Access **tabs**; the prototype lays it out as **KPI MetricCards + a Members & access table + an All-suites table** (no tabs). Reconcile `Admin.tsx` to the prototype layout (prototype wins) on the existing `/admin/{suites,users,access}` endpoints — presentation only, no new backend. _Design ref: `templates/app/AdminScreen.jsx`._

**Week 6 total: 2 / 27** _(early credit: Results page scaffold + severity badge colours (PR-C1, the GX-Cloud redesign Phase C); 3 dashboard tasks partially landed — status/provider filters, per-check drill-down, pipeline-runs feed. Prototype-adoption now the **full screen set** (10 tasks, ADR 0022): nav/shell, Dashboard landing, suite-create page, edit-flows→pages, results drill-down route, error pages, **+ New-Connection source-select split, Profile content, Settings page (moved in from W7), Admin layout-reconcile**. +1 moved in from Week 5: recent-runs audit table — folded into the Dashboard page.)_

content.text: Now the Week-7 side — remove Settings (moved to W6) and update its counts:

content.content:
343	### Prototype adoption — backend-gated screens + final polish (added — not in original roadmap) (5 tasks — 0/5) — tracked in epic [#176](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/176)
344	> Closes out the prototype after the Week-6 structural pass: the two screens that need backend (**Admin**, **Settings**) plus the final visual-fidelity pass to the design-system guidelines. Admin needs a real **workspace-admin** authz concept (today `admin` is per-suite only) — the prototype's client-side "View as" switch is **not** used (real authz is server-side, CLAUDE.md §11). **To open the prototype, see the *Design source* note in the Week-6 prototype-adoption group** (project `317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d` via the `DesignSync` tool).
345	- [x] ✅ **Backend: workspace-admin list-all endpoint** — **shipped early in Week 4 (#289)** to unblock the Admin page. `GET /admin/suites` · `/admin/users` · `/admin/access` behind a **workspace-admin** authz check (config `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` allowlist, distinct from the per-suite `view < edit < admin < owner` ladder; no migration). Deliberately unscoped (bypasses owned-or-shared). `/me` exposes `is_workspace_admin`.
346	- [x] ✅ **Admin control centre page** (`/admin`) — **shipped early in Week 4 (#289)**. Suites / Users / Access tabs on the new endpoint, with a **real 403** `Forbidden` page for non-admins (server-driven via `/me`'s `is_workspace_admin` — *not* a client "View as" toggle), admin-only nav, shared `MeProvider`/`useMe`.

Backend-gated. _Design ref: `templates/app/SettingsScreen.jsx`._
348	- [ ] ⬜ **Dashboard KPI honesty pass** — the Dashboard renders only metrics with a real data model: pass-rate, run counts, and `metric_value`-backed trends (ADR 0012 seam). **Omit "anomalies detected"** (the `anomaly` monitor-kind is reserved, not implemented in v1) and **"avg. time to resolution"** (there is no incident/resolution model). Re-introduce when the monitor kinds / model exist (post-v1 Theme A). _Design ref: `templates/app/DashboardScreen.jsx` (the KPI card row — keep pass-rate / runs / trends, drop the two unbacked KPIs)._

Admin needs a real **workspace-admin** authz concept (today `admin` is per-suite only) — the prototype's client-side "View as" switch is **not** used (real authz is server-side, CLAUDE.md §11). **To open the prototype, see the *Design source* note in the Week-6 prototype-adoption group** (project `317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d` via the `DesignSync` tool).
- [x] ✅ **Backend: workspace-admin list-all endpoint** — **shipped early in Week 4 (#289)** to unblock the Admin page. `GET /admin/suites` · `/admin/users` · `/admin/access` behind a **workspace-admin** authz check (config `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` allowlist, distinct from the per-suite `view < edit < admin < owner` ladder; no migration). Deliberately unscoped (bypasses owned-or-shared). `/me` exposes `is_workspace_admin`.
- [x] ✅ **Admin control centre page** (`/admin`) — **shipped early in Week 4 (#289)**. Suites / Users / Access tabs on the new endpoint, with a **real 403** `Forbidden` page for non-admins (server-driven via `/me`'s `is_workspace_admin` — *not* a client "View as" toggle), admin-only nav, shared `MeProvider`/`useMe`.
- [ ] ⬜ **Workspace Settings page** (`/settings`, `SettingsScreen`) — the app-shell settings screen. **No settings/preferences backend exists today** (only the `v1/` routers), so v1 ships the page shell + only controls that map to a real backend (e.g. Teams alert webhook config once the Week-6 `ResultPublisher` notification-config UI lands); everything else renders as a clearly-labelled placeholder until a settings API exists. Backend-gated. _Design ref: `templates/app/SettingsScreen.jsx`._
- [ ] ⬜ **Dashboard KPI honesty pass** — the Dashboard renders only metrics with a real data model: pass-rate, run counts, and `metric_value`-backed trends (ADR 0012 seam). **Omit "anomalies detected"** (the `anomaly` monitor-kind is reserved, not implemented in v1) and **"avg. time to resolution"** (there is no incident/resolution model). Re-introduce when the monitor kinds / model exist (post-v1 Theme A). _Design ref: `templates/app/DashboardScreen.jsx` (the KPI card row — keep pass-rate / runs / trends, drop the two unbacked KPIs)._
- [ ] ⬜ **Visual-fidelity pass to the design spec** — self-hosted **Inter** + **JetBrains Mono** `@font-face` (if not already wired), motion tokens (120–180ms ease on bg/border/shadow, 0.5px press nudge), the 2px indigo focus ring, hover elevation on interactive cards, and empty/loading states — matched to the prototype's `guidelines/*` foundation cards. Keep using **semantic tokens** so post-v1 dark mode inherits for free. _Design ref: `guidelines/{type-body,type-mono,color-indigo,color-neutrals,color-status,spacing-scale,elevation,radius}.card.html` + `tokens/{fonts,typography,elevation,spacing,colors}.css` + `assets/fonts/`; readme §"Visual foundations" / "Motion" / "Focus" / "Iconography"._

**Week 7 total: 2 / 34** _(+5 prototype-adoption tasks: workspace-admin endpoint ✅ + Admin page ✅ both shipped early in Week 4 via #289; remaining: Settings page, Dashboard KPI honesty pass, visual-fidelity pass)_

{"body":"Tracking epic for **Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting**. Mirrors the roadmap; the task-level source of truth remains [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md). Milestone: `Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting`.

**In progress — 2 / 23** (early credit: Results page scaffold + severity badge colours, PR-C1).

**Results dashboard**
- [ ] Health score stat cards + 7-day trend chart
- [ ] Per-suite pass / fail progress bars — updated to show warn / fail / critical breakdown
- [ ] Results filter bar — by env, datasource type, suite, date range, status (incl. warn / fail / critical) _(status + provider filters shipped PR-C1; rest pending)_
- [ ] Failed check drill-down — sample failing rows from GX result _(per-check drill-down shipped PR-C1; sample rows pending PII redaction #226)_
- [ ] Per-check historical trend chart (not just overall health score)
- [ ] ADF pipeline status panel — polls every 30s; webhook-pushed failures + polling-filled succeeded runs; correlated DQ result _(pipeline-runs feed shipped PR-C1; 30s poll + correlation pending)_
- [ ] Datasource type filter — toggle Snowflake / flat file / Unity Catalog on results view
- [ ] CSV + PDF export of results
- [ ] **Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results** — _moved from Week 5_ (epic #174). The cross-suite recent-runs widget on the C0b read API (`GET /runs`), each row deep-linking to the run-detail route. **Built once in its final home:** its page-home is the Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard (`/dashboard`, below — which already lists a recent-runs table among its widgets) and its drill-down target is the routed run-detail page (`/results/:runId`, #268); both are W6 tasks and the dashboard gates on a chart-library decision, so a throwaway standalone table on `/results` in W5 was avoided.
- [x] Severity badge colours — green (pass), amber (warn), red (fail), dark red (critical) — applied across result tables/drill-downs (PR-C1)
- [ ] Health score weighting — apply agreed penalty weights (warn / fail / critical) in the health-score calc; severity breakdown in the 7-day trend tooltip

**Alerting**
- [ ] Notification config UI — Teams webhook per suite, alert on fail / warn / always
- [ ] Alert suppression / snooze — silence a specific check for N hours
- [ ] Alert dedup — fire on first failure only, not on every subsequent scheduled run
- [ ] Teams adaptive card payload — check name, datasource, table / file, observed vs expected
- [ ] Severity-aware alert routing — `warn` quieter, `fail` standard, `critical` @channel + distinct styling

**Prototype adoption — dedicated-page flows + Dashboard landing (added — not in original roadmap)**
> DataQ Design System prototype adoption; mirrors the matching group in [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md) (the task-level source of truth).
- [ ] Nav / shell restructure — Dashboard landing entry + reordered sider + footer group (Settings · Admin · Documentation); login redirect `/` → `/dashboard`
- [ ] Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard page (`/dashboard`, `DashboardScreen`) — hosts the Week-6 dashboard widgets **incl. the moved-in recent-runs table**; prereq: a chart-library decision
- [ ] New Suite → dedicated page (`/suites/new`, `SuiteFlow`)
- [ ] Edit flows → dedicated pages (connection / suite / check `:id` routes; removes the edit drawers)
- [ ] Results drill-down → dedicated route (`/results/:runId`, `ResultsScreen` detail) — #268
- [ ] Standard error pages (4xx / 5xx) — `ErrorState` in the app shell + router catch-all

_Severity badge colours (✅) + the Results page scaffold landed early in PR-C1 (the GX-Cloud redesign Phase C)._","number":175,"title":"Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting (tracking epic)"}
===== 176 =====
{"body":"Tracking epic for **Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs**. Mirrors the roadmap; the task-level source of truth remains [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md). Milestone: `Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs`.


**DevOps & deployment**
- [ ] Containerise FastAPI + React + Celery + Redis
- [ ] Push images to Azure Container Registry
- [ ] Deploy to Azure Container Apps (API + Celery worker) + Azure Static Web App (React UI)
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline — lint, test, build, deploy on merge to `main`
- [ ] Application Insights integration — traces, errors, slow queries, Celery task metrics

**Azure Monitor webhook setup (one-time infra, post-deployment)**
- [ ] Create Action Group (pre-prod) — type: Webhook, URL: `https://<pre-prod-api>.azurecontainerapps.io/api/v1/adf/events`, shared secret: value from Key Vault
- [ ] Create Alert Rule (pre-prod) — scope: `example-adf-preprod` factory, signal: `Failed pipeline runs`, dimension: all pipelines (tick "include all future values"), action: Action Group above
- [ ] Create Action Group (prod) — same config pointing to prod API URL
- [ ] Create Alert Rule (prod) — scope: `example-adf-prod` factory, same signal + dimension config
- [ ] Smoke test — trigger a deliberate pipeline failure in DEV, confirm webhook fires, DB updates, and ADF panel reflects failure within 5 min

**FastMCP — MCP server**
- [ ] Install FastMCP, scaffold `mcp_server.py`, mount MCP app into FastAPI at `/mcp` — `app.mount("/mcp", mcp.get_asgi_app())`
- [ ] Wire Azure AD token validation into FastMCP's auth provider — validate Bearer token on every MCP tool call using the same `verify_azure_ad_token` logic as the REST API
- [ ] Implement `list_suites` resource — all suites accessible to current user, name / datasource / env / last run status / check count
- [ ] Implement `get_suite_results` resource — latest DQ run results for a suite; pass/fail per check, observed vs expected values, sample failing rows
- [ ] Implement `get_health_score` resource — overall health score and 7-day trend, filterable by env and datasource type
- [ ] Implement `get_adf_pipeline_status` resource — latest ADF pipeline run status with correlated DQ result per pipeline
- [ ] Implement `trigger_suite_run` tool — async GX suite execution against a given datasource + env; returns `run_id` for polling
- [ ] Implement `get_run_status` tool — poll live check-by-check progress for a running execution using `run_id`
- [ ] Implement `create_check` tool — add a new GX expectation to a suite (form path: check type + column + threshold; or raw SQL)

Scope locked by ADR [0022](docs/adr/0022-week6-prototype-adoption-and-chart-library.md): adopt the **full 13-screen prototype set** as dedicated, deep-linkable pages — **the Share drawer is the only surviving drawer; the prototype wins on any drawer-vs-page / layout conflict**. Plus the **alerting** track — `ResultPublisher` seam (ADR 0011) + Teams notification config / suppression / dedup / severity-aware routing. Live warehouse/file run remains the deferred Week-1 smoke.

docs/progress.md:176:### Connection manager UI (6 tasks — 6/6 ✅)
docs/progress.md:238:> The roadmap's 5th Execution-UI task — **recent-runs audit table** — was **moved to Week 6** (counted there). Why: the [DataQ Design System](#week-6--results-dashboard--alerting) prototype makes the **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard** (`/dashboard`, the [Week-6 task](#week-6--results-dashboard--alerting)) the page-home for the recent-runs widget, and its row drill-down target is the Week-6 routed run-detail page (`/results/:runId`, [#268](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/268) — promotes `Results.tsx`'s in-page `openRun` state to a route). Building a standalone table on `/results` now and rebuilding it on `/dashboard` in W6 is throwaway work; the dashboard also gates on the chart-library decision first. Deferred to build it **once, in its final home** — see Week 6.
docs/progress.md:264:- [ ] ⬜ **Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results** _(moved from Week 5 — see that week's Execution-UI note)_ — the cross-suite recent-runs widget on the C0b read API (`GET /runs`), each row deep-linking to the run-detail route. **Why deferred from W5:** its page-home is the Week-6 **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard** (`/dashboard`, prototype-adoption task below — which already lists a recent-runs table among its hosted widgets), and its drill-down target is the Week-6 routed run-detail page (`/results/:runId`, [#268](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/268)). content.text:
Done. Week 6 scope is now fully assessed, the decision is captured in an ADR, and every tracking doc + GitHub epic is in sync.

## What I did

**Pulled & read the whole prototype** — all 13 screens (`templates/app/*` + `templates/error/`) plus the canonical `readme.md`. Confirmed it's a *structure* adoption (foundation already matches our `theme.ts`/`resultsFormat.ts`), with the teal `DESIGN.md` correctly ignored.

**Wrote [ADR 0022](docs/adr/0022-week6-prototype-adoption-and-chart-library.md)** — your two decisions:
- **Chart library = recharts** (lightweight, MIT, lazy-loaded so it ships only on chart routes; clears `pnpm audit` + bundle gate)
- **Full 13-screen prototype adoption in Week 6** — every create/edit flow becomes a dedicated page, **Share is the only surviving drawer**, **prototype wins on any drawer-vs-page/layout conflict**. Records what's *not* adopted (View-as switch, marketing, dark mode).

**Reconciled the docs to the true scope** (this is the "wasn't documented properly" gap):
- Week 6: **23 → 27 tasks**. The prototype-adoption group went 6 → 10 by adding the three under-tracked screens — **Profile content, New-Connection source-select split, Admin layout-reconcile** — plus **Settings pulled in from Week 7**.
- Week 7: **34 → 33** (Settings moved out; the two admin tasks ticked as already-shipped via #289).
- Grand total **178 → 181** (net +3 genuinely new; Settings is a move).
- Updated: ADR index, CLAUDE.md §9 decision table + §13 milestone, progress.md (headline, both weeks, aggregate table, the `>100` note).
- Synced GitHub epics **#175** (Week 6) and **#176** (Week 7) to mirror.

Nothing is committed — these are working-tree changes to `docs/adr/0022-*.md`, `docs/adr/README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/progress.md` (epics #175/#176 are already live on GitHub).

## Where we stand — the Week-6 build queue

The full screen set, in a sensible build order (each ships as its own commit per the working agreement):

1. **Foundation:** install recharts; **nav/shell restructure** (add Dashboard, footer group, `/`→`/dashboard`) — unblocks everything
2. **`/results/:runId` route** (drawer → page + CSV/JSON download) — the recent-runs drill-down target
3. **`/dashboard`** landing — KPI cards, Quality Trends, Suite Performance bars, recent-runs table
4. **Drawer → page restructures:** New Suite, then edit flows (connection/suite/check), New-Connection source-select split
5. **Profile content**, **Settings**, **Admin reconcile**
6. **Error pages** (4xx/5xx)
7. **Alerting track** (backend): `ResultPublisher` seam → Teams config → suppression/dedup/routing

Want me to kick off with step 1 (recharts + nav/shell), or would you like to review the ADR/doc changes first?

input.content:
# Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting · working TODO

> **Local scratch — gitignored** (`*_TODO.md`). Source of truth stays [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md) + epic [#175](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/175). This file is the *execution plan*: ordering, file pointers, acceptance, commit boundaries. Update freely; don't commit.

**Exit gate:** Full results dashboard live across all source types; alerts firing with suppression.
**Scope:** 27 tasks (ADR [0022](docs/adr/0022-week6-prototype-adoption-and-chart-library.md)). Full 13-screen prototype set as dedicated pages — **Share is the only drawer; prototype wins on any drawer-vs-page / layout conflict.** Chart lib = **recharts** (lazy-loaded). Currently 2/27 (Results scaffold + severity colours, PR-C1).

**Working rules (CONTRIBUTING):** one functionality per commit · manually test before next · feature branch off `main` · conventional commits · `/code-review` agents before merge · run format/lint/typecheck/bandit/tests locally before push · fixup commits on open PRs (no amend+force) · file defects as issues (`Fixes #N`) with the W6 milestone.

**Prototype source:** project `317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d` via `DesignSync` (`method=get_file path=templates/app/<Screen>.jsx`). `readme.md` = canonical spec. Build in real **antd** reading `theme.ts` — the `.jsx` is reference only.

---

## Phase 0 — Foundation (unblocks everything)

### 0.1 Install recharts + chart wrapper  ▸ commit `feat(frontend): add recharts + lazy chart primitives`
- [ ] `pnpm add recharts` in `frontend/`; confirm it lazy-loads (dynamic import on chart routes only, like the `lazy()` route split in [App.tsx](frontend/src/App.tsx#L16))
- [ ] Run `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` + check the bundle delta — record in the PR body (ADR 0022 gate)
- [ ] Thin wrapper `components/charts/` reading colours from `theme.ts` (no hardcoded hex) — pass/fail/warn/critical from `resultsFormat.ts` maps
- [ ] **Verify:** a throwaway chart renders with brand colours; `/dashboard` chunk is separate in `pnpm build`

### 0.2 Nav / shell restructure  ▸ commit `feat(frontend): dashboard nav + footer group + /dashboard landing`
- [ ] [App.tsx](frontend/src/App.tsx): `NAV_ITEMS` → **Dashboard · Connections · Suites · Results · Profile**; footer group **Admin · Settings · Documentation** (Documentation = placeholder)
- [ ] Login redirect `/` → `/dashboard` (was `/connections`); `*` catch-all → error page (see 6.1) once it exists, else `/dashboard`
- [ ] Register routes: `/dashboard`, `/results/:runId`, `/suites/new`, `/connections/:id/edit`, `/suites/:id/edit`, `/suites/:id/checks/:checkId/edit`, `/settings`
- [ ] Admin/Settings only in footer when `useIsWorkspaceAdmin()` (nav convenience; route still registered)
- [ ] _Ref:_ `AppShell.jsx` (white header + light sider + footer nav)
- [ ] **Verify:** every new route resolves (stub pages ok); nav highlight prefix-matches sub-routes; non-admin doesn't see Admin in nav

---

## Phase 1 — Results run-detail route (drawer → page)

### 1.1 `/results/:runId` page  ▸ commit `feat(frontend): routed run-detail page replacing the drawer`
- [ ] Promote the `openRun` `useState` ([Results.tsx:54](frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx#L54)) → routed `RunDetail` page; remove the Drawer
- [ ] `PageHeader` (back → Results) · status/checks-passed/started/duration MetricCards · per-check results table (check + expectation mono, severity tag, `metric_value`, observed via `ScalarValue`)
- [ ] Row click in `RunsTab` → `navigate(\`/results/${runId}\`)`; deep-linkable + refreshable (fetch by id on mount via `getRun`)
- [ ] **Sample failing rows stay withheld** (PII, [#226](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/226))
- [ ] _Ref:_ `ResultsScreen.jsx` → `RunDetailScreen`

### 1.2 CSV / JSON download  ▸ commit `feat(frontend): CSV/JSON export on run detail`
- [ ] Download menu (CSV + JSON) on run-detail header, reusing [utils/download.ts](frontend/src/utils/download.ts)
- [ ] CSV: check, expectation, status, metric, observed (proper escaping); JSON: run meta + checks array
- [ ] _Ref:_ `DownloadMenu` in `ResultsScreen.jsx`
- [ ] **Verify:** both files download with correct content; filename slug from suite name

> Covers W6 tasks: *Results drill-down → dedicated route* + *CSV/PDF export* (CSV/JSON now; PDF optional/deferred — note in PR).

1	import type { ThemeConfig } from 'antd';
2	
3	/**
4	 * App-wide Ant Design theme. Kept in one module so the brand palette and the
5	 * shell (App.tsx) read from the same tokens rather than hard-coding hexes.
6	 *
7	 * Design intent: move off the stock antd "navy-header admin template" look.
8	 * An indigo primary + a soft gray layout canvas (`colorBgLayout`) give the
9	 * white surfaces (header, sider, cards) depth and definition — so the page no
10	 * longer reads as a flat sea of white, and the empty space frames content
11	 * instead of just being blank.
12	 */
13	
14	export const BRAND = {
15	  /** Indigo-600 — the primary accent (buttons, active nav, links). */
16	  primary: '#4f46e5',
17	  /** Indigo-200 — the logo's light lobe + the watermark tint. */
18	  primarySoft: '#c7d2fe',
19	  /** Pale indigo — the "selected" row/nav background (one tint everywhere). */
20	  selectedBg: '#eef0fe',
21	  /** The soft canvas behind white surfaces. */
22	  canvas: '#f4f5f7',
23	  /** Hairline border for header / sider / cards. */
24	  border: '#e6e8eb',
25	  /** Primary text. */
26	  ink: '#1f2430',
27	} as const;
28	
29	/** Shared shell metrics so App.tsx and the theme agree. */
30	export const SHELL = {
31	  headerHeight: 56,
32	  siderWidth: 220,
33	} as const;
34	
35	export const appTheme: ThemeConfig = {
36	  token: {
37	    colorPrimary: BRAND.primary,
38	    colorInfo: BRAND.primary,
39	    colorLink: BRAND.primary,
40	    colorTextHeading: BRAND.ink,
41	    colorBgLayout: BRAND.canvas,
42	    borderRadius: 8,
43	    fontFamily:
44	      "'Inter', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif",
45	    fontSize: 14,
46	  },
47	  components: {
48	    Layout: {
49	      headerBg: '#ffffff',
50	      headerHeight: SHELL.headerHeight,
51	      headerPadding: '0 24px',
52	      siderBg: '#ffffff',
53	      bodyBg: BRAND.canvas,
54	    },
55	    Menu: {
56	      // Rounded, inset nav items read as a modern sidebar rather than full-bleed rows.
57	      itemBorderRadius: 8,
58	      itemMarginInline: 8,
59	      itemHeight: 38,
60	      itemSelectedBg: BRAND.selectedBg,
61	      itemSelectedColor: BRAND.primary,
62	    },
63	    Card: {
64	      borderRadiusLG: 12,
65	    },
66	    Table: {
67	      headerBg: '#fafbfc',
68	    },
69	  },
70	};
71

1	# CLAUDE.md — DataQ project guide for AI assistants
2	
3	> Single source of truth for any Claude / AI assistant working in this repo. Read this end-to-end before touching code.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. Project summary
8	
9	**DataQ** is a single-tenant data quality monitoring platform built around Great Expectations (GX Core). It runs DQ checks across **4 datasources** and integrates with **2 orchestration providers**.
10	
11	| Layer | Components |
12	|---|---|
13	| **Datasources (you can write checks against)** | Snowflake (DEV/QA/UAT), ADLS Gen2, AWS S3, Unity Catalog (Databricks) |
14	| **Orchestration providers (monitor + trigger only — NOT datasources)** | Azure Data Factory (ADF), Apache Airflow |
15	| **Backend** | FastAPI + Celery + Redis + PostgreSQL + Alembic |
16	| **Frontend** | React + Vite + Ant Design + Monaco editor |
17	| **Auth / secrets** | Azure AD (MSAL) + Azure Key Vault |
18	| **Deploy** | Azure Container Apps + Azure Static Web App |
19	| **Observability** | Azure Application Insights + structlog |
20	| **AI integration** | FastMCP (8 curated tools mounted at `/mcp`) — Claude Desktop / Claude.ai / Copilot / Cursor |
21	
22	Timeline: **8 weeks** to v1. Scope: single tenant, suite-level access sharing, Azure-hosted.
23	
24	---
25	
26	## 2. Architecture at a glance
27	
28	See [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) for the full diagram (Mermaid — renders on GitHub).
29	
30	```
31	Browser ──HTTPS──► React (Static Web App) ──► FastAPI (Container Apps) ──► PostgreSQL
32	                                                    │  │
33	AI clients ──MCP/HTTP──► FastAPI /mcp endpoint      │  └──► Celery worker ──► GX execution ──► Snowflake / ADLS / S3 / UC
34	                                                    │
35	                                                    ├──► Redis (task queue)
36	                                                    ├──► Key Vault (secrets)
37	                                                    └──► App Insights (observability)
38	
39	ADF ──► Azure Monitor alert rule ──► webhook ──► POST /api/v1/orchestration/events/adf
40	Airflow ──► on_success/on_failure_callback ──► POST /api/v1/orchestration/events/airflow
41	FastAPI ──► MS Teams webhook (alerts)
42	```
43	
44	---
45	
46	## 3. Repo layout
47	
48	Flat monorepo (decided in Week 1):
49	
50	```
51	DataQ/
52	├── backend/                     # FastAPI + Celery + GX (Python, conda)
53	│   ├── app/
54	│   │   ├── core/                # logging, errors, config (locked in PR 2)
55	│   │   ├── db/                  # SQLAlchemy models, session
56	│   │   ├── api/                 # FastAPI routers (versioned: /api/v1/...)
57	│   │   ├── services/            # business logic per domain
58	│   │   ├── orchestration/       # OrchestrationProvider abstraction (ADF, Airflow)
59	│   │   ├── datasources/         # ConnectionAdapter + CheckRunner per type; gx_runner.py (shared GX translation), flatfile.py (flat-file IO + runner + batch resolution)
60	│   │   └── mcp/                 # FastMCP tools (Week 7)
61	│   ├── alembic/
62	│   └── tests/                   # + tests/support/ (adversarial harness), tests/integration/ (end-to-end datasource runs)
63	├── frontend/                    # React + Vite + Ant Design (Node, pnpm)
64	│   ├── src/
65	│   └── tests/
66	├── docs/
67	│   ├── architecture.md          # Mermaid architecture diagram
68	│   └── adr/                     # Architecture Decision Records
69	├── integrations/                # user-deployed snippets (NOT app code; e.g. Airflow DAG callback)
70	│   └── airflow/                 # dataq_airflow_callback.py + setup README
71	├── scripts/
72	│   └── setup.sh                 # one-command dev env bootstrap
73	├── context/                     # original product/roadmap context (read-only reference)
74	│   └── DataQ_platform_roadmap.md
75	├── .github/
76	│   ├── workflows/
77	│   ├── pull_request_template.md
78	│   ├── CODEOWNERS
79	│   └── ISSUE_TEMPLATE/
80	├── docker-compose.yml
81	├── environment.yml              # conda env — pip section points at backend/requirements-dev.txt
82	├── pyproject.toml               # Black + Ruff + mypy config
83	├── CONTRIBUTING.md
84	├── CLAUDE.md                    # this file
85	└── README.md
86	```
87	
88	**Promotion to `apps/` + `packages/`:** only if a real shared package emerges (e.g., auto-generated OpenAPI client in Week 4–5). Default flat.
89	
90	---
91	
92	## 4. Datasources vs orchestration — critical distinction
93	
94	**Datasources** are stores you write DQ checks against:
95	- Snowflake (DEV/QA/UAT)
96	- ADLS Gen2 (flat files)
97	- AWS S3 (flat files)
98	- Unity Catalog / Databricks
99	
100	**Orchestration providers** are NOT datasources. They are workflow engines whose pipelines/DAGs we observe and react to. Their *only* three responsibilities in DataQ:
101	
102	1. **Monitor** pipeline/DAG runs → stored in `pipeline_runs` table (separate from `runs` / `results`).
103	2. **Detect failure** in near-real-time via provider-specific event channels (webhook for both).
104	3. **Trigger suite execution on successful completion** via `trigger_bindings` (`provider`, `pipeline_or_dag_id`, `suite_id`, `env`). Failure events alert the user but do NOT trigger suite runs.
105	
106	Both providers implement a single `OrchestrationProvider` interface — ADF is the reference implementation, Airflow is the second. **Never hardcode ADF-only logic; always go through the abstraction.**
107	
108	| Provider | Event channel | Auth | Polling fallback |
109	|---|---|---|---|
110	| ADF | Azure Monitor alert → webhook | Shared secret header (Azure Monitor's only mode) | ADF REST API, 10 min |
111	| Airflow | DAG `on_*_callback` → webhook | HMAC-signed payload (signing key in Key Vault) | Airflow REST API `dagRuns`, 10 min |
112	
113	Airflow callbacks require the user to add a snippet to their DAGs (we can't mutate them). Polling is the documented fallback.
114	
115	**Anti-pattern (do not do this):** treating ADF/Airflow as a 5th/6th datasource in the connection editor, check editor, or suite model.
116	
117	---
118	
119	## 5. Framework choice — GX-only for v1
120	
121	- **v1:** Great Expectations (GX Core) is the sole DQ framework across all 4 datasources. Unifies result schema, suite/check model, MCP tools, and the check editor. Every v1 check is a GX **expectation** (`check.kind = 'expectation'`).
122	- **v1.1:** Databricks Labs **DQX** will be added for DLT / streaming use cases (GX is batch-only and runs poorly on streaming). DQX will implement the same `UnityCatalogCheckRunner` interface introduced in Week 3 — UI exposes `engine: gx | dqx` toggle on UC suites.
123	- **Monitor-kind seam (do-now, Week 3):** not every monitor is a GX expectation. A `check.kind` discriminator (`expectation` in v1; `freshness | volume | schema_drift | anomaly | comparison` reserved) + numeric `metric_value` on results let v1.x auto-monitors slot in without a check/result schema rewrite. This seam is **orthogonal to the datasource seams** (`CheckRunner`, `ConnectionAdapter`): it varies by *monitor kind*, not datasource. See ADR `0012` (and `0014` for the reserved `comparison` / cross-dataset reconciliation kind) and post-v1 roadmap Theme A. Most real incidents are freshness/volume, not value-level — this is the leap from "GX runner" to DQ platform.
124	- **Week-3 outcome (done):** the UC run path is thin behind `UnityCatalogCheckRunner` (reads the table into a GX DataFrame asset — the DQX swap-in shape), and `check.kind` + `metric_value`/`duration_ms` shipped in the one threshold migration, so the monitor-kind impls won't ripple into the suite/check/result layer later.
125	
126	---
127	
128	## 6. Working agreements (rules above feature work)
129	
130	Full list (37 rules across 8 categories) lives in [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md). Highlights:
131	
132	### Commit & change discipline
133	- **One functionality per commit** (where possible).
134	- **Manually test each committed change before starting the next functionality** (required until unit tests land in Week 8).
135	- **Defects → GitHub issue, never silent fixes.** Use `gh issue create`. The fixing PR must include `Fixes #N`.

... [184 lines truncated] ...

1	# Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
2	
3	Each ADR captures a single significant architecture decision: the context, the decision, the consequences, and the alternatives considered. New ADRs are append-only — supersede an old decision by adding a new ADR and marking the old one's status as `Superseded by ADR-NNNN`.
4	
5	## Format
6	
7	- File name: `NNNN-short-kebab-slug.md` (zero-padded 4-digit sequence)
8	- Frontmatter fields: Status, Date, Deciders
9	- Sections: Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives considered, Related (optional)
10	- Keep each ADR short — 1–2 pages. If it grows past that, the decision is probably two decisions.
11	
12	## Index
13	
14	| # | Title | Status |
15	|---|---|---|
16	| [0001](0001-trunk-based-branching.md) | Trunk-based branching with squash-merge into `main` | Accepted |
17	| [0002](0002-conventional-commits.md) | Conventional commits for PR titles and commit messages | Accepted |
18	| [0003](0003-gx-only-for-v1.md) | GX-only for v1; DQX deferred to v1.1 for DLT/streaming | Accepted |
19	| [0004](0004-orchestration-abstraction.md) | Unified `OrchestrationProvider` abstraction for ADF and Airflow | Accepted |
20	| [0005](0005-severity-tier-weights.md) | Severity tier weights (warn / fail / critical → health score) | Accepted |
21	| [0006](0006-adf-webhook-authentication.md) | ADF webhook authentication (shared secret in URL + hard-cutover rotation) | Accepted |
22	| [0007](0007-airflow-callback-model.md) | Airflow callback model (HMAC-signed webhook + polling fallback) | Accepted |
23	| [0009](0009-flat-monorepo-layout.md) | Repo layout — flat monorepo (`backend/` + `frontend/`) | Accepted |
24	| [0010](0010-provider-agnostic-infrastructure-seams.md) | Provider-agnostic infrastructure seams (Azure is the default, not the architecture) | Accepted |
25	| [0011](0011-extensibility-seams-for-deferred-integrations.md) | Extensibility seams for deferred connectors and integrations | Accepted |
26	| [0012](0012-monitor-kind-seam.md) | Monitor-kind seam (`check.kind` discriminator + numeric metric storage) | Accepted |
27	| [0013](0013-marketplace-distribution-and-anti-lock-in.md) | Marketplace distribution (customer-deployed BYOL) and anti-vendor-lock-in guardrails | Accepted |
28	| [0014](0014-reconciliation-comparison-check-kind.md) | Cross-dataset reconciliation as a `comparison` check kind (reuse FastAPI_DataComparison engine) | Accepted |
29	| [0016](0016-severity-derivation-semantics.md) | Severity derivation semantics (band the unexpected-%, thresholds override GX success) | Accepted |
30	| [0017](0017-python-313-runtime-upgrade.md) | Upgrade Python runtime 3.11 → 3.13 (3.14 deferred — GX-capped); bundled with the Snowflake 3→4 CVE refresh | Accepted |
31	| [0018](0018-results-surface-and-grafana-deferral.md) | Results surface is an in-app page (suite-scoped authz + PII redaction); Grafana deferred to optional ops add-on | Accepted |
32	| [0019](0019-custom-sql-check-kind.md) | Custom-SQL checks ride `kind='expectation'` via GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation` (no new kind); read-only validation + SQL-datasource gating | Accepted |
33	| [0020](0020-history-and-audit-strategy.md) | History/audit: per-entity Type-4 snapshot tables (`check_versions`, `connection_versions`) where config history is needed; no SCD-2; credentials never snapshotted; cascade-delete accepted; cross-entity audit log deferred | Accepted |
34	| [0021](0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md) | Live test/demo-data environment (retail model, 3 reference flows) lives outside the repo — Terraform/mock-data/Databricks notebook not git-tracked; discharges the deferred live-warehouse/file smoke | Accepted |
35	
36	## Pending (to be written in their respective weeks)
37	
38	| # | Topic | Target week |
39	|---|---|---|
40	| 0008 | MCP mounted at `/mcp` with Azure AD auth | Week 7 |
41	| 0015 | Two-connection check model (source + target refs for `comparison` checks) | When reconciliation build starts (post-v1) |

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(frontend): add recharts + lazy chart primitives

Foundation for the Week-6 dashboard charts (ADR 0022): recharts 3.8.1
(pnpm audit --audit-level=high clean), plus two primitives every chart
widget will mount through —

- chartTheme.ts: result-severity + run-status → hex series colours
  (the chart-domain mirror of resultsFormat.ts's antd Tag names, since
  SVG needs concrete hex), the indigo/grid/axis tokens, and shared
  axis/grid/tooltip style. Record<…> maps so a new status is a compile
  error, not a silently grey series.
- ResponsiveChart.tsx: one height/empty-state contract; the only place
  recharts is imported, so it stays out of the initial bundle and ships
  only on the lazy chart routes.

4 unit tests for the colour maps. format/lint/typecheck/test green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -6
